text.title: Webinar Delivery Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Webinar Delivery application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Host MUST present with camera on; audio-only delivery is rejected.
rule.rationale: Face-on lifts retention measurably; audio-only delivery feels like a phone hold.

rule.statement: Engagement event (poll / question / chat prompt) MUST fire every ≤7 minutes. Lecture-only blocks are rejected.
rule.rationale: Attention spans collapse past 7 minutes of monologue; engagement events reset the curve.

rule.statement: Time-limited offer MUST surface during Q&A, not at the end. Offer-at-the-end is rejected.
rule.rationale: End-of-webinar audiences shrink ~30%; Q&A is the highest-attention window for a sales ask.

rule.statement: Follow-up MUST be a 5-email sequence over 7 days. Single thank-you email is rejected.
rule.rationale: Single-email follow-ups under-convert by 60-80% vs sequences; the offer needs multi-touch.

rule.statement: Recording MUST yield ≥10 derivative assets (clips, posts, transcript, lead-magnet). Single-recording publishing is rejected.
rule.rationale: One-hour webinar effort produces single-channel use; repurposing extends shelf life by months.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
